% Source data: scripts/figures/load_imbalance_table.csv
% Generated by scripts/make_load_imbalance_table.py
\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Effect of load-balancing on three SpMM algorithms.
\textbf{(a)} Device throughput (TFLOP/s) on triangular BSR matrices
($M{=}N{=}K$, $R{=}C{=}256$), where the per-row work is intentionally
imbalanced. \textbf{(b)} Host-side runtime per iteration (ms) on a random
25\% density workload where work is already balanced. The \emph{LB speedup}
columns show how much faster the load-balanced variant is than the
no-LB variant of the same algorithm; values $>1$ mean LB is faster,
$<1$ mean LB hurts.}
\label{tab:load_imbalance}
\small

\textbf{(a) Triangular load imbalance} \\[2pt]
\begin{tabular}{l@{\,\,}l rrrr rrrr}
\toprule
 & & \multicolumn{4}{c}{TFLOP/s} & \multicolumn{4}{c}{LB speedup} \\
\cmidrule(lr){3-6} \cmidrule(lr){7-10}
 & & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Lower-tri} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Upper-tri} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Lower-tri} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Upper-tri} \\
\cmidrule(lr){3-4} \cmidrule(lr){5-6} \cmidrule(lr){7-8} \cmidrule(lr){9-10}
Algo. & Var. & 8192 & 4096 & 8192 & 4096 & 8192 & 4096 & 8192 & 4096 \\
\midrule
Naive & LB    & 19.1 & 18.8 & 19.1 & 19.0 & 0.81 & 0.81 & 0.89 & 0.94 \\
      & no-LB & 23.6 & 23.3 & 21.5 & 20.1 &      &      &      &      \\
\addlinespace[2pt]
SnF   & LB    & 40.1 & 38.4 & 40.1 & 38.6 & 0.96 & 0.97 & 0.88 & 0.89 \\
      & no-LB & 41.9 & 39.7 & 45.7 & 43.2 &      &      &      &      \\
\addlinespace[2pt]
DDA   & LB    & 43.3 & 39.5 & 29.6 & 29.5 & \textbf{1.47} & \textbf{1.24} & \textbf{1.19} & \textbf{1.10} \\
      & no-LB & 29.5 & 31.8 & 24.9 & 26.8 &      &      &      &      \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}

\vspace{8pt}

\textbf{(b) Random 25\% density workload (host-side ms / iteration)} \\[2pt]
\begin{tabular}{l rrr}
\toprule
Algorithm & LB (ms) & no-LB (ms) & LB speedup \\
\midrule
Naive & 18.01 & 18.27 & 1.015 \\
SnF   &  9.93 &  9.66 & 0.973 \\
DDA   &  7.73 &  8.32 & \textbf{1.076} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
